senses.definition: to be able to pay the cost of something

examples.x: If you save consistently, you may eventually afford a house.
Budgeting wisely helps individuals afford their desired lifestyle without overspending.
Individuals are affording education through various financial planning strategies.
The goal is to afford essential needs and still have room for savings and leisure activities.
Financial stability allows individuals to afford unexpected expenses without causing hardship.

senses.definition: the money that is returned to us when we have paid more than the actual cost of something

examples.x: When I handed the server a twenty-dollar bill for my meal, I waited patiently for my change to be returned.
As I paid for my bus fare with a larger bill, the driver handed me my change along with the ticket.
The store clerk apologized for the inconvenience and promptly provided me with the correct change after realizing they had overcharged me for the item.
After paying for my groceries, I received my change from the cashier, including a few coins and a dollar bill.

senses.definition: to ask a person to pay a certain amount of money in return for a product or service

examples.x: The hotel may charge for additional services beyond the room rate.
Some online platforms charge for premium features.
Restaurants in tourist areas often charge more for their meals.
Airlines may charge extra for checked baggage.
The event organizers decided to charge for entry to cover expenses.

senses.definition: a piece of metal, typically round and flat, used as money, issued by governments

examples.x: She found a rare coin from the 19th century while cleaning out her grandfather's attic.
The vending machine only accepts coins, so he had to dig through his pockets to find some change.
Each country has its own unique designs on their coins, reflecting its culture and history.
Collecting coins has become a popular hobby for many people who enjoy discovering their value and rarity.
The government decided to issue a new coin to commemorate the upcoming national holiday.

senses.definition: the type or system of money that is used by a country

examples.x: The euro is the official currency of many countries in the European Union.
The value of the local currency fluctuates based on the global market.
Using a stable currency is crucial for maintaining economic stability.
Travelers often need to exchange their currency at the airport.
The value of the currency dropped significantly after the announcement.

senses.definition: the amount of money that is reduced from the usual price of something

examples.x: They offered a discount of 20% on all winter clothing.
The store gave a discount of $10 on purchases over $50.
With the discount applied, the final price was much more affordable.
The store provided a 15% discount for first-time customers.

senses.definition: having a reasonable price

examples.x: The restaurant offers inexpensive meals that are still delicious.
The store sells inexpensive furniture that is perfect for students on a budget.
She prefers to buy inexpensive cosmetics rather than splurging on high-end brands.
The hotel offers inexpensive room rates during off-peak seasons.
They bought an inexpensive used car to save money on transportation.
She found an inexpensive dress that still looked stylish.

senses.definition: ‌a large building or enclosed area, where many stores are placed

examples.x: We spent the entire afternoon shopping at the mall.
The new mall downtown features over 200 retail stores.
They opened a gourmet food court in the center of the mall.
During the holidays, the mall is decorated with festive lights and ornaments.
The mall offers a wide variety of stores, from high-end boutiques to budget-friendly shops.

senses.definition: a stand or a small table or shop with an open front where people sell their goods

examples.x: He set up a small stall to sell handmade jewelry.
The flower stall on the corner always has the best roses.
Street vendors filled the square, each running a different stall.
A book stall at the festival offered rare first editions.
She helped her mother manage their vegetable stall at the farmers’ market.

senses.definition: to bring back a purchased item to the seller in order to receive a refund

examples.x: After discovering a defect in the new electronic gadget, Sarah decided to return it to the store for a refund.
The shoes didn't fit properly, so Mark had to return them.
Jane received a duplicate gift and decided to return one of them to the department store for a refund.
The store's generous return policy allowed customers to return clothing items within 30 days.
The customer realized that the color of the paint didn't match the sample, so they decided to return it.

senses.definition: a person who wants to buy something, usually an expensive item

examples.x: The buyer inspected the car before making the purchase.
She is the buyer for a major retail store.
Online buyers often leave reviews about their purchases.
The company is looking for a buyer for its old equipment.
A buyer’s satisfaction is crucial for repeat business.

senses.definition: a person or company that sells something
someone who goes to shops or online platforms to buy something

examples.x: The shopper carefully inspected each item on the shelves before selecting the perfect gift for her friend's birthday.
As a seasoned shopper, Sarah knew all the best places to find discounts and deals during the holiday season.
The shopper browsed through the online catalog, adding items to their cart with each click of the mouse.
With a list in hand, the shopper navigated the aisles of the grocery store, ticking off items as they went.
The shopper appreciated the convenience of online shopping, allowing them to compare prices and read reviews from the comfort of their home.

senses.definition: something that is created or grown for sale

examples.x: The bakery's best-selling product is its freshly baked bread.
The company introduced a new product line of organic skincare products.
Farmers in the region grow a variety of agricultural products, including fruits and vegetables.
Consumers are increasingly demanding eco-friendly products.
The tech startup launched its flagship product at the trade show last month.

senses.definition: items made or produced for sale

examples.x: The store offers a wide range of goods, from fresh produce to handmade crafts.
During the festival, local artisans displayed their goods in colorful booths for everyone to browse.
The shipment of goods arrived late, causing a delay in restocking the shelves.
Online shopping has made it easier than ever to purchase goods from around the world.
He decided to donate his gently used goods to charity, hoping to help those in need.

senses.definition: a place in a supermarket where people pay for the goods they buy

examples.x: At the checkout, the cashier scanned each item with precision, ensuring an accurate total.
The line at the checkout stretched all the way to the back of the store during the busy holiday season.
As I reached the checkout, I realized I had forgotten to grab a carton of milk from the dairy section.
Self-service checkouts have become increasingly popular, allowing customers to scan and pay for their items independently.
After waiting patiently in line, I finally reached the checkout and paid for my groceries with a credit card.

senses.definition: an arrangement according to which a bank keeps and protects someone's money that can be taken out or added to

examples.x: John transferred money from his checking account to his investment account to buy stocks.
The company's accountant meticulously balanced the accounts to ensure accuracy in financial records.
Tom received an email notification confirming that his account had been credited with the refund amount.

senses.definition: to have the responsibility of paying someone back a certain amount of money that was borrowed

examples.x: I owe my friend $50 from last month when he covered my dinner expenses.
She realizes she owes her sister a significant sum after borrowing money for car repairs.
We owe the bank a monthly mortgage payment for our home loan.
After borrowing funds for tuition, I now owe the student loan company a substantial amount.
We owe a repayment to the neighbor who lent us money during a financial setback.

senses.definition: the ability to buy something from a shop or receive money from a bank based on trust, without paying for it immediately
an amount of money or a favor that is owed

examples.x: After years of diligent saving, he finally managed to pay off his student debt.
They were struggling under the weight of mounting debt and needed to seek financial advice.
She felt a deep sense of debt to her mentor, who had guided her throughout her career.
The company's debt continued to grow as profits failed to meet expectations.
He repaid his friend, feeling relieved to be free of the personal debt he had owed for so long.

senses.definition: to freely give goods, money, or food to someone or an organization

examples.x: Individuals often donate clothing to local shelters during the winter months.
The company decided to donate a portion of its profits to support environmental causes.
Many people donate to food banks to help those facing hunger.
The philanthropist donated a significant amount to fund medical research.
The community raised funds to donate to a family in need during challenging times.

senses.definition: the sum of money that is left in a bank account

examples.x: After paying bills and expenses, she always checks her balance to ensure she has enough money left for savings.
When making large purchases, it's crucial to keep an eye on your balance to avoid overdrawing your account.
He checked his balance online to confirm that his paycheck had been deposited.
The ATM displayed his current balance before he decided to withdraw cash.
She was pleasantly surprised to see her balance increase after receiving a refund for an overcharged bill.

senses.definition: the amount of money spent to do or have something

examples.x: Keeping track of your monthly expenses can help you create a realistic budget.
Unexpected car repairs can often be a significant expense for many people.
Rent and utilities are among the most common monthly expenses for homeowners and renters alike.
When planning a trip, it's essential to consider all potential expenses, including accommodations, transportation, and meals.
Many people use budgeting apps to categorize their expenses and identify areas where they can cut back to save money.

senses.definition: the worth of something in money

examples.x: He estimated the value of his car before selling it.
The value of the antique vase was estimated at $5000.
The real estate agent emphasized the value of the property's location in determining its market price.
The company's stocks plummeted, causing shareholders to worry about the value of their investments.
She questioned the value of the expensive handbag, wondering if it was worth the price.

senses.definition: a total of money, typically owed in a financial transaction

examples.x: The sum owed on the invoice amounted to $500.
After adding up all the expenses, the total sum came to $1000.
He withdrew a large sum from his savings account to purchase a car.
She transferred a considerable sum of funds to her investment portfolio.

senses.definition: the whole amount of something
an item bought at a much lower price than usual

examples.x: She found a great bargain on shoes during the sale.
The jacket was a real bargain at half the original price.
Bargains like these are hard to come by in high-end stores.
The handbag was a bargain considering its brand and quality.
The used car was a bargain compared to newer models.

senses.definition: a sum of money that has to be paid, based on one's income, to the government so it can provide people with different kinds of public services

examples.x: The government imposed a new tax on luxury goods to increase revenue.
The company hired a tax consultant to ensure compliance with the latest tax laws and regulations.

senses.definition: (usually plural) anything that a person has or owns at a specific time

examples.x: As he packed his possessions into boxes, memories flooded back from years spent in the old house.
Ownership of the house was transferred to her name, making it her possession.
The thief was caught red-handed with stolen possessions in his backpack.
His possessions included a vintage record player, a collection of antique books, and a cherished family heirloom.
Losing her possessions in the fire was devastating, but she was grateful that her family was safe.

senses.definition: to be one's property
an amount of money not spent

examples.x: Growing your own vegetables can lead to a saving in grocery expenses during the harvest season.
His decision to buy a used car instead of a new one led to a significant saving in terms of money.

senses.definition: the act or process of transforming raw materials or different components into goods that can be used by customers

examples.x: The factory increased its production of cars to meet rising demand.
The company invested in new machinery to streamline the production process.
Sustainable practices were implemented to make the production of goods more eco-friendly.
The production of the new smartphone model will start next month.
The film studio announced the production of a new blockbuster movie.

senses.definition: equal to a specified amount of money, etc.

examples.x: The necklace is worth $500.
The painting is worth a fortune.
The vacation package is worth the price.
The vintage watch is worth its weight in gold.
He received a promotion that doubled his salary, making the job worth the effort.
The car is worth $10,000 according to the appraisal.

senses.definition: a reduction in something such as size, amount, etc.

examples.x: She made a significant cut to her expenses by canceling unnecessary subscriptions and memberships.
The company announced a workforce cut, resulting in layoffs and job losses.
He implemented a strict calorie cut to achieve his weight loss goals.

senses.definition: great pleasure and comfort afforded by expensive food, places, or clothes

examples.x: The designer dress she wore was the epitome of luxury.
The car’s luxury features included heated seats and a premium sound system.
The weekend retreat was a true luxury, with gourmet meals and spa treatments.
Dining at the five-star restaurant was an unparalleled luxury.
The cruise offered pure luxury, from the lavish rooms to the fine dining.

senses.definition: expensive in way that is not reasonable

examples.x: The hotel stay felt overpriced given its poor facilities.
The overpriced coffee shop had few customers during the week.

senses.definition: a unit of currency or coin used in several countries, equal to one hundredth of a dollar or pound

examples.x: He found a lucky penny on the sidewalk.
I have a penny that was minted in the year I was born.
I picked up a penny off the ground.
She tossed a penny over her shoulder into the pool for luck.
The loaf of bread cost eighty pennies.

senses.definition: ‌a printed form that we can write an amount of money on, sign, and use instead of money to pay for things

examples.x: He received a check for the sale of his old car.
I wrote a check to my child's school for the field trip fee.
The plumber preferred payment by check.
The restaurant doesn't accept checks, only cash or cards.

senses.definition: the act of selling something

examples.x: Following the sale of her novel, she became a well-known author.
He made a lot of profit from the sale of his paintings.
The sale of the old car gave him enough money to buy a new bike.
The sale of the old family house made everyone nostalgic.
Their family’s main income comes from the sale of farm produce.
